text:
I implemented `topoSort` in `toposort.js` using Kahn's algorithm. It is fully general — no hardcoding or special-casing.

How it works:
- Builds an adjacency list and in-degree count over every node in `nodes`, plus any node that appears only inside `edges` (so nothing is dropped).
- Each `[a, b]` edge adds `b` to `a`'s adjacency and increments `b`'s in-degree, enforcing "a before b".
- Seeds a queue with all zero-in-degree nodes, repeatedly emits one and decrements its successors' in-degrees, appending each successor when it hits zero.
- If the emitted order doesn't cover every node, the leftover nodes (in-degree still > 0) form a cycle, so it throws an `Error` naming them.

This returns an ordering of all nodes that respects every edge, and throws on any cycle including self-loops.

One caveat: I could not execute my verification test. Running an actual `node` script is held behind a permission approval in this environment that didn't get granted (benign calls like `node --version` and `ls` pass, but executing a script file does not). So the code is written and reviewed but **not run-verified by me**. If you run it, this one-liner exercises the main paths:

```bash
node -e 'const t=require("./toposort.js");
console.log(t(["a","b","c","d"],[["a","b"],["a","c"],["b","d"],["c","d"]]));
try{t(["a","b","c"],[["a","b"],["b","c"],["c","a"]])}catch(e){console.log("cycle ->",e.message)}'
```

Decision trail:
- Chose Kahn's (BFS in-degree) over DFS post-order because cycle detection falls out naturally from the "not all nodes emitted" check; both are valid, Kahn's keeps the throw logic simpler — see `toposort.js:43`.
- Included nodes that appear only in `edges` via the `ensure()` helper (`toposort.js:13`) rather than assuming `nodes` is exhaustive, so edge-only nodes still get ordered.
- Not verified / where to look: I never executed the test — the `node` script run was blocked by an unapproved permission prompt, so correctness rests on code review only; run the snippet above to confirm, with attention to the cycle-throw and the all-nodes-covered (`order.length !== adj.size`) branch at `toposort.js:43`.
